skills.name: native-feel-cross-platform-desktop
skills.description: Architecture and audit guidance for cross-platform desktop apps that must feel native across macOS and Windows, covering native shell boundaries, system WebView usage, typed IPC, memory trade-offs, and native-feel conventions.
recommendedFor: Designing or rewriting cross-platform desktop apps that need a native feel
Evaluating Electron, Tauri, WebView, or native-shell architecture trade-offs
Auditing WebView-based desktop UI polish, launch feel, memory, IPC, and platform conventions
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements skill-recommender as a third-party candidate for desktop architecture and native-feel UI decisions; it is not a replacement for risk-oriented-code-review.

skills.name: ponytail
skills.description: Minimalist engineering guidance that pushes agents toward YAGNI, reuse, standard library and native platform features, smallest correct diffs, and over-engineering review/audit workflows.
recommendedFor: Keeping implementation scope minimal without removing required safety checks
Reviewing diffs for over-engineering, speculative abstractions, redundant dependencies, and avoidable boilerplate
Auditing a repository for code that can be deleted, simplified, or replaced with standard library or native platform features
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements risk-oriented-code-review by focusing on over-engineering and unnecessary complexity rather than correctness, security, or compatibility risk; useful as a third-party candidate for skill-recommender.

skills.name: reverse-skill
skills.description: Cybersecurity skill router and tool-orchestration pack for authorized reverse engineering, penetration testing, CTF, APK/binary/JS analysis, firmware work, report generation, and field-journal based knowledge reuse.
recommendedFor: Routing authorized reverse-engineering, CTF, APK, binary, frontend JS, packet-capture, firmware, and security-testing tasks to the right workflow
Bootstrapping or checking local security tooling such as jadx, apktool, Frida, radare2, IDA-related tooling, BurpSuite integrations, and browser automation
Generating security analysis reports, attack-path diagrams, CTF writeups, and reusable field-journal notes after authorized work
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements skill-recommender as a third-party candidate for authorized security and reverse-engineering workflows; it is broader and more toolchain-oriented than risk-oriented-code-review.

skills.name: ppt-master
skills.description: AI-driven presentation workflow that converts source documents, URLs, Markdown, existing PPTX decks, or topic briefs into real editable PowerPoint files through SVG generation, quality checks, post-processing, templates, notes, narration, and PPTX export.
recommendedFor: Creating a real editable PowerPoint deck from PDF, DOCX, URL, Markdown, text, or a topic brief
Reusing or filling an existing PPTX template while keeping native PowerPoint editability
Beautifying an existing deck, generating speaker notes or narration, and exporting native PPTX rather than slide images
skills.relationshipToFirstPartySkills: Complements skill-recommender as a third-party candidate for PPT generation and deck workflows; it is a specialized artifact-generation workflow rather than a code review skill.
